results.title: Commerce announces Summer in the City event, holiday closures
results.description: The City of Commerce will host its annual Summer in the City Celebration on Saturday, June 27. The event will feature activities throughout the day, culminating with a fireworks display

results.title: Maryland mailed absentee ballots to a convicted illegal immigrant -- for years
results.description: Maryland mailed absentee ballots to an illegal immigrant for years -- and resisted removing him even after his arrest and guilty plea. Here's what the records show.

results.title: Woman kicked in face, beaten and severely injured in Lawrence, police say
results.description: Two people were found dead in a rural area of west Benton County Saturday evening after a car crash and then gunshots fired in an apparent domestic violence incident.

results.title: Florida ranks high for elder fraud as issue affects over 88,000 a year
results.description: The number of older adults scammed out of $10,000 or more in the US has more than quadrupled since 2020, according to the Federal Trade Commission.

results.title: Newsom, Lawmakers Agree on $11 Billion Housing Bond for November Ballot
results.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom and the Legislature announced Monday that they have agreed to place an $11.25 billion affordable housing and homeownership bond on the November ballot. The Veterans and Affordable Housing Bond Act of 2026 would fast-track the construction, rehabilitation, and preservation of affordable housing, support homeownership programs, and provide funding for a variety of state [...] The post Newsom, Lawmakers Agree on $11 Billion Housing Bond for November Ballot appeared first on GV Wire .

results.title: “They couldn’t afford me, they had to kill me off” – Deyemi Okanlawon jokes about ‘Blood Sisters’ exit
results.description: Nigerian actor Deyemi Okanlawon has jokingly attributed his early exit from the Netflix series Blood Sisters to his alleged high fees, saying his character was written out because the production could not afford him. Speaking in a light-hearted conversation with Ghanaian media personality Doreen Avio on the sidelines of the 2026 Ghana Movie Awards, the actor [...] The post “They couldn’t afford me, they had to kill me off” – Deyemi Okanlawon jokes about ‘Blood Sisters’ exit appeared first on Ghana News Online .

results.title: 95 villages in Cagayan, Batanes at risk due to Francisco
results.description: TUGUEGARAO CITY, CAGAYAN, Philippines — At least 95 villages in Batanes and Cagayan provinces are at risk of flooding and landslides as Typhoon Francisco (international name: Mekkhala) moves closer to Northern Luzon, prompting government agencies to intensify disaster preparedness measures.
